title: Prunus henryi
intro: Prunus henryi, also known as the Henry's cherry or Chinese flowering cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to China. The health benefits of Prunus henryi include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Prunus henryi is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Prunus henryi is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a type of cherry tree native to China. Prunus henryi is classified within the genus Prunus, which includes other species of stone fruits. The genus Prunus is part of the subfamily Amygdaloideae, a group of plants that also includes apricot and plum trees. This classification places Prunus henryi in the larger order Rosales.
uses: Prunus henryi is a flowering plant native to China, and its leaves and bark have been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. Prunus henryi is also used to treat fever and rheumatism due to its supposed analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's leaves are used to treat gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and constipation. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Prunus henryi for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Prunus henryi contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins. These compounds are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal properties and antioxidant activities. side_effects: Prunus henryi, also known as Chinese cherry or Henry's cherry, is a flowering plant native to China.
